WCAG 2.4.4 		Check that the link text combined with programmatically determined link context identifies the purpose of the link.
Link Purpose (In Context)
The purpose of each link must be clear from the link text alone, or from the link text together with its context.
Impact: Screen reader users navigating a list of links cannot determine where "click here" goes.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > body > main > section:nth-child(2) > div > div > div:nth-child(2) > div > div:nth-child(1) > div > div:nth-child(3) > div > a
https://www.kidhora.it/ristoranti/osteria-lon-cha-i-e/
2 errors 4 warnings 9 notices ▼
Severity WCAG Criterion What's Wrong Legal Impact Element
notice 2.4.2 — Page Titled
WCAG 2.4.2 		Check that the title element describes the document.
Page Titled
Web pages must have descriptive, meaningful titles.
Impact: Users navigating with browser tabs or screen readers cannot identify pages without titles.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > head > title
notice 1.1.1 — Non-text Content
WCAG 1.1.1 		Ensure that the img element's alt text serves the same purpose and presents the same information as the image.
Non-text Content
All images, icons, and non-text content must have text alternatives that describe their purpose.
Impact: Screen reader users cannot understand what images convey without alt text.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA #header-logo-69bbea238081c
notice 1.1.1 — Non-text Content
WCAG 1.1.1 		If this image cannot be fully described in a short text alternative, ensure a long text alternative is also available, such as in the body text or through a link.
Non-text Content
All images, icons, and non-text content must have text alternatives that describe their purpose.
Impact: Screen reader users cannot understand what images convey without alt text.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA #header-logo-69bbea238081c
warning 1.4.3 — Contrast (Minimum)
WCAG 1.4.3 		This element's text or background contains transparency. Ensure the contrast ratio between the text and background are at least 4.5:1.
Contrast (Minimum)
Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background (3:1 for large text).
Impact: Users with low vision or color blindness cannot read text with insufficient contrast.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > body > header > nav > div > div:nth-child(1) > a > noscript
error 1.4.3 — Contrast (Minimum)
WCAG 1.4.3 		This element has insufficient contrast at this conformance level. Expected a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1, but text in this element has a contrast ratio of 4.45:1. Recommendation: change background to #fafbfc.
Contrast (Minimum)
Text must have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 against its background (3:1 for large text).
Impact: Users with low vision or color blindness cannot read text with insufficient contrast.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > body > main > section:nth-child(1) > div > nav > ol > li:nth-child(4)
warning 2.5.3 — Label in Name
WCAG 2.5.3 		Accessible name for this element does not contain the visible label text. Check that for user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually.
Label in Name
For UI components with visible text labels, the accessible name must contain the visible text.
Impact: Voice control users who speak the visible label cannot activate controls with mismatched accessible names.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > body > main > section:nth-child(2) > div > div > div:nth-child(1) > div:nth-child(1) > div > div:nth-child(2) > button:nth-child(1)
notice 3.2.1 — On Focus
WCAG 3.2.1 		Check that a change of context does not occur when this input field receives focus.
On Focus
Receiving keyboard focus must not trigger an unexpected change of context.
Impact: Keyboard and screen reader users are disoriented when focusing an element causes page changes.
 EAA WAD Section 508 ADA Equality Act PSBAR BITV 2.0 RGAA DDA ACA html > body > main > section:nth-child(2) > div > div > div:nth-child(1) > div:nth-child(1) > div > div:nth-child(2) > button:nth-child(1)
warning 2.5.3 — Label in Name
WCAG 2.5.3 		Accessible name for this element does not contain the visible label text. Check that for user interface components with labels that include text or images of text, the name contains the text that is presented visually.
Label in Name
For UI components with visible text labels, the accessible name must contain the visible text.
Impact: Voice control users who speak the visible label cannot activate controls with mismatched accessible names.
